commands.review_summary:
The PR rejects unsafe decimal `Content-Length` values in the OpenAI chat-tools E2E client and hardens Docker heartbeat tests by separating numeric normalization from timing-sensitive heartbeat proof.

PR surface: Tests +13, Other 0. Total +13 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main's guard only rejects safe integer declared lengths above the cap, and the PR body/comment reports a direct mock gateway repro where an unsafe decimal header now fails fast instead of timing out.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair job is needed; the remaining action is maintainer landing judgment for a protected maintainer PR.

target.title: fix(e2e): reject unsafe chat tools body lengths
target.body:
## Summary

- Reject unsafe numeric `Content-Length` values in the OpenAI chat tools client before waiting on the response stream.
- Add regression coverage for unsafe declared chat completion body lengths.
- Harden Docker E2E heartbeat coverage by separating `08` normalization from real-time heartbeat timing.

## Verification

- `node --check scripts/e2e/lib/openai-chat-tools/client.mjs`
- Direct mock gateway repro: `Content-Length: 9007199254740993` with `OPENCLAW_OPENAI_CHAT_TOOLS_MAX_BODY_BYTES=64` fails in ~150ms with `chat completions response body exceeded 64 bytes`.
- Docker heartbeat shell proof for `run_logged_print_heartbeat plugins-run 1 ... /bin/sleep 4`.
- Docker heartbeat normalization shell proof for `docker_e2e_normalize_positive_int_value 'Docker E2E log heartbeat interval' 08` -> `8`.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` clean, overall patch correct `0.88`.
- CI release gate for `c483c6e6921b9608f27e9f93edf2e1a9e4a9d18b`: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/27843455246 succeeded.

## Notes

The first exact-head release gate exposed a brittle Docker heartbeat timing test in `checks-node-core-tooling-docker`. The second commit removes that timing boundary from the zero-padded normalization assertion; the rerun passed the previously failing lane and the full release gate.
target.author: vincentkoc
